results.title: Evolution Championship Series - Wikipedia
results.content:
EVO 2026 will see the Extended Lineup and Throwback Tournament being scrapped in place of the main lineup featuring twelve games, with the six highest-entered games being featured on the arena stage. The year's lineup will include Street Fighter 6, Tekken 8, Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, Under Night In-Birth II [Sys:Celes], Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising, Rivals of Aether II, Invincible VS, Vampire Savior, Guilty Gear -STRIVE-, BlazBlue: Central Fiction, Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O. World Stage, and 2XKO.

## Outside Vegas

[edit] [...] EVO 2025 was held from August 1 to August 3, with a lineup of Street Fighter 6, Tekken 8, Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising, Guilty Gear -Strive-, Under Night In-Birth II [Sys:Celes], Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves, and Mortal Kombat 1, with Marvel vs. Capcom 2 being featured as the Throwback Tournament. Additionally, 2025 rebranded the Evo Community Showcase to the Evo Extended Lineup, a series of side tournaments officially organized and recognized by Evo despite not being featured in the main arena lineup. The Extended Lineup included Killer Instinct "Killer Instinct (2013 video game)"), Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O., Rivals of Aether II, The King of Fighters XV, BlazBlue: Central Fiction, Guilty Gear Xrd REV2, Samurai Shodown "Samurai Shodown (2019 video game)") and Capcom vs. SNK 2. The [...] Evo 2013 was announced on December 2, 2012. It was held on July 12–14 at Paris Las Vegas. On January 8, Joey Cuellar announced that EVO 2013 would have eight main tournament games, with the first seven revealed being Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3, Super Street Fighter IV: Arcade Edition (Version 2012), Tekken Tag Tournament 2, Mortal Kombat "Mortal Kombat (2011 video game)"), Street Fighter X Tekken (Version 2013), The King of Fighters XIII, and Persona 4 Arena. The eighth game was voted on by players via online donations, all proceeds of which went to the Breast Cancer Research Foundation. The eventual winner of the poll was Super Smash Bros. Melee with $94,683 raised for that game, while the total amount of donations was $225,744. Nintendo of America initially sent a cease and desist

results.title: What is Evo like? (2025 edition) - YouTube
results.content: Battle by the Bay which has three B's in it. Hence its alternate name B3 which is kind of like E3. Welcome to B3. While EVO nowadays is like an E3 for fighting games. B3 was a Street Fighter tournament with just over 100 players where the NorCal organizers begged arcades to borrow cabinets to play at a mini golf center in Sunnyvale, California. The following tournament was called B4, then B5, and in 2002, the tournament was finally called the Evolution Championship Series. At this point, Japanese players were regularly coming to compete. And in 2004, Daigo Umahara's Justin Wong, giving birth to Moment 37, bringing new eyes into the scene. In order to scale up, Evbo moved to Las Vegas the very next year. It was a more neutral territory amidst the rivalry between the east and west coasts, [...] what really happened. And for the biggest matches like the Sunday Finals, you're basically in a stadium arena. It's like a giant concert except that it's the best fighting game players in the world fighting for the most coveted title in the FGC. Every finals I've been to have had mind-blowing moments that make the crowd lose their minds. If you've ever been hype watching a moment on stream, being there in person will feel like it's over 9,000. When watching the FGC from afar, it's easy to forget that it's a real community with real people. When you get there, you'll see these people in person and not just their internet persona. The open bracket format means you might get a chance to play them in a match and you won't forget it. The matches you play offline in a tournament like EVO get [...] amidst the rivalry between the east and west coasts, but also doubled as a tourist destination for anyone traveling. Going to Evo now meant going to Las Vegas, which is a city that literally calls itself the entertainment capital of the world. And this makes it much easier to convince your friends to go with you. Panina coladas on a sunbed next to a swimming pool is what got my better half to come with me. And if you like shows, I recommend Cir to Sole, which is basically like watching a 20v20 tag fighter. But being a city full of attractions means it has crazy good event infrastructure, ones that can accommodate thousands of people with air conditioning. What comes with the venue that can accommodate over 25,000 people is a viewing experience to match that. The screens are huge, and the

results.title: Welcome to Evo 2025: A First-Timer’s Guide to the Fighting Game Experience | Evo | The Ultimate Fighting Game Tournament
results.content:
You’ll also be able to find fighting game themed backdrops and photo areas around the venue, so choose your stage, strike a pose and get to say cheese, I mean fight.

# The Finals Arena: Fighting Game Theater

On Saturday and Sunday, the energy shifts. The lights go down, the crowd packs in, and the best players in the world step onto the Evo Finals stage. These are the Top 8s—the climactic showdowns that crown champions. Whether you’re screaming in the front row or watching the big screen, it is an emotional ride and the ultimate celebration of competitive greatness. [...] The arena is free to enter all day on Saturday, however will require an arena ticket on Sunday. If you can’t make it to the arena, have no fear, the Top 8s will be watchable from the convention floor with large scale screens and in-house commentators of its own.

# Tips for First-Timers [...] # Preparing for EVO 2025: Pre‑Trip Checklist

Whether you’re a seasoned fighter or a hype newcomer, organizing your essentials early ensures you can focus on the matches, not the logistics.

1. Badge & Registration

 Secure your badge via the official Evo Start.gg page. Registration is essential for access to the entire event. Double check you have your correct tournament entry and your Arena Finals ticket if you want to get into the Evo
 Arena on Sunday (currently sold out in 2025).
 After purchase, double‑check your Start.gg account or email for badge pick‑up instructions and hours.

2. Flights & Travel
